name: Tokelau (New Zealand)

colors.symbolism: the four stars representing the Southern Cross constellation

description: A blue field charged with a stylised yellow Tokelauan canoe (vaka) sailing toward four white five-pointed stars in the upper hoist, arranged as the Southern Cross.
history: Adopted on 7 September 2009 following a community-driven design competition. Tokelau is a non-self-governing territory of New Zealand consisting of three atolls; the flag was approved by the General Fono and given royal assent by Queen Elizabeth II.

features.symbolism: the ocean surrounding the three atolls of Tokelau
a stylised yellow outrigger canoe sailing toward the Southern Cross; represents the navigational journey of Tokelau toward self-determination while keeping ties to family, culture, and the nation.
the Southern Cross constellation, which guides the canoe; represents Tokelau's geographical position in the South Pacific.
